---
title: "between_timepoint_wilcoxon_d10_vs_d5_codon"
subtitle: "ribostall stall_sites — enrichment stats"
date: 2026-05-12
format:
  html:
    toc: true
    toc-depth: 3
    embed-resources: true
    fig-cap-location: bottom
  pdf:
    toc: true
    fig-cap-location: bottom
    include-in-header:
      text: |
        \usepackage{needspace}
tbl-colwidths: [22, 8, 18, 17, 17, 26]  # aa-col-added
execute:
  echo: false
---

## Composite figure

![Three-panel bar plot (A-site, P-site, E-site, left-to-right) showing per-codon log2 fold-change of day_10-vs-day_5 median per-replicate frequency, with BWM and control reps pooled within each timepoint, across the 61 sense codons. Within each panel bars are sorted by log2_FC high-to-low; blue = enriched at day_10 (log2_FC >= 0), red = depleted at day_10 (log2_FC < 0). Y-axis (`log2 Fold-Change`) spans ~-1.1 to +0.65.](../../plots/between_timepoint/d10_vs_d5/codon/composite/APE_codon_Day10_vs_Day5_barplot_composite.png)

Each panel ranks the 61 sense codons by signed log2_FC (day_10 median / day_5 median) at one ribosome site. Bars above zero are day_10-enriched relative to day_5; bars below zero are day_10-depleted.

---

## Overview

- **CSV:** `between_timepoint_wilcoxon_d10_vs_d5_codon.csv`
- **Family (from `_INDEX.md`):** `between_timepoint_wilcoxon`
- **Sister CSV(s):**
  - `between_timepoint_wilcoxon_d10_vs_d5_aa.csv`
  - `between_timepoint_wilcoxon_d10_vs_d0_aa.csv`
  - `between_timepoint_wilcoxon_d10_vs_d0_codon.csv`
  - `between_timepoint_wilcoxon_d5_vs_d0_aa.csv`
  - `between_timepoint_wilcoxon_d5_vs_d0_codon.csv`
- **Test:** Mann-Whitney U / Wilcoxon rank-sum (two-sided)
- **Design:** n=4 day_10 (BWM_d10_rep2/3 + control_d10_rep2/3) vs n=4 day_5 (BWM_d5_rep2/3 + control_d5_rep2/3); BWM and control reps pooled inside each timepoint; two-sided MW on per-replicate codon frequencies; BH-FDR per A/P/E site (each site = own family of 61 sense codons); effect column is `log2_FC` = log2(median_d10 / median_d5).

### Family-level caveats

- **mw-floor-blocking** — MW two-sided p-floor for n=4 vs n=4 is exactly 0.02857; with per-site BH-FDR families of ~20 (aa) or ~61 (codon), clearing FDR<0.05 is mathematically impossible unless ~every test ties at the floor. Empirically: 0 hits at FDR<0.05 in any of the 6 files.
- **condition-pooled-confound** — n=4 per timepoint is built by pooling 2 BWM + 2 control reps; if BWM and control diverge in their time response, pooling masks divergent signals. All 6 members share this design.
- **n=4-low-power** — n=4 vs n=4 MW has very low power; no-signal results across all 6 files are weakly informative.
- **p-floor-aware-headline** — Headlines must explicitly state min raw p ~= 0.0286 and explain why "no FDR hits" here is uninformative, not a biological negative.

---

## Headline

No statistically significant differences at FDR<0.05 (0/183) for codon-level day_10 vs day_5 MW with BWM and control reps pooled within each timepoint (n=4 per side). Min p_adj = 0.436 at site A — driven by 4 floor rows (CAA -0.681, TTT -0.465, GGA +0.317, TCT -0.281), giving BH wall 61 x 0.0286 / 4 = 0.436 (matches observed). Total floor rows file-wide: 7 (4 at A, 1 at E (ATT -0.304), 2 at P (CTT -0.189, GGA +0.366)). Site E (min p_adj 0.871) and site P (min p_adj 0.581) are flatter than A. Closest-to-significant codon-level signature: at A site, CAA / TCT / TTT depletion + GGA enrichment in day_10 vs day_5 — i.e. day_10 reps trend toward more A-site GGA (Glycine) and less A-site CAA (Glutamine), TCT/TTT (Ser/Phe). Treat as exploratory; the floor blocks formal FDR<0.05 here regardless of biology.

## Top hits

Effect column is `log2_FC` (day_10 / day_5 median ratio). `p_value` is the raw Mann-Whitney p; `p_adj` is BH-corrected per A/P/E site (family of 61 codon tests). Each site group is split into one sub-table per sign of effect: positive `log2_FC` means day_10-enriched; negative means day_10-depleted. Within each sub-table, rows are up to 5 per direction ranked by raw `p_value` ascending, with `|log2_FC|` descending as the tiebreaker. The `low-count` flag is applied when `min(median_day_10, median_day_5) < 0.005` (verifiable via `python scripts/dylan_table_checker.py <CSV> --family wilcoxon`). The `aa` column is the single-letter amino acid translation of each codon.

```{=latex}
\needspace{14\baselineskip}
```

### A site — Enriched (day_10 > day_5)

| codon | aa  | effect (`log2_FC`) | raw p (`p_value`) | adjusted p (`p_adj`) | flag      |
| ----- | --- | ------------------ | ----------------- | -------------------- | --------- |
| GGA   | G   | +0.317             | 0.0286            | 0.436                | floor     |
| CGT   | R   | +0.367             | 0.0571            | 0.498                |           |
| CGC   | R   | +0.250             | 0.1143            | 0.697                |           |
| TGG   | W   | +0.175             | 0.1143            | 0.697                |           |
| ACC   | T   | +0.197             | 0.2000            | 0.871                |           |

```{=latex}
\needspace{14\baselineskip}
```

### A site — Depleted (day_10 < day_5)

| codon | aa  | effect (`log2_FC`) | raw p (`p_value`) | adjusted p (`p_adj`) | flag             |
| ----- | --- | ------------------ | ----------------- | -------------------- | ---------------- |
| CAA   | Q   | -0.681             | 0.0286            | 0.436                | floor            |
| TTT   | F   | -0.465             | 0.0286            | 0.436                | floor, low-count |
| TCT   | S   | -0.281             | 0.0286            | 0.436                | floor            |
| AAA   | K   | -0.388             | 0.0571            | 0.498                |                  |
| ACT   | T   | -0.301             | 0.0571            | 0.498                |                  |

```{=latex}
\needspace{14\baselineskip}
```

### P site — Enriched (day_10 > day_5)

| codon | aa  | effect (`log2_FC`) | raw p (`p_value`) | adjusted p (`p_adj`) | flag      |
| ----- | --- | ------------------ | ----------------- | -------------------- | --------- |
| GGA   | G   | +0.366             | 0.0286            | 0.581                | floor     |
| ACC   | T   | +0.281             | 0.0571            | 0.581                |           |
| AGG   | R   | +0.371             | 0.1143            | 0.757                | low-count |
| GAG   | E   | +0.197             | 0.2000            | 0.813                |           |
| TGC   | C   | +0.151             | 0.2000            | 0.813                |           |

```{=latex}
\needspace{14\baselineskip}
```

### P site — Depleted (day_10 < day_5)

| codon | aa  | effect (`log2_FC`) | raw p (`p_value`) | adjusted p (`p_adj`) | flag      |
| ----- | --- | ------------------ | ----------------- | -------------------- | --------- |
| CTT   | L   | -0.189             | 0.0286            | 0.581                | floor     |
| CAA   | Q   | -0.556             | 0.0571            | 0.581                |           |
| ATT   | I   | -0.476             | 0.0571            | 0.581                |           |
| TCT   | S   | -0.304             | 0.0571            | 0.581                |           |
| TTT   | F   | -0.659             | 0.1143            | 0.757                | low-count |

```{=latex}
\needspace{14\baselineskip}
```

### E site — Enriched (day_10 > day_5)

| codon | aa  | effect (`log2_FC`) | raw p (`p_value`) | adjusted p (`p_adj`) | flag      |
| ----- | --- | ------------------ | ----------------- | -------------------- | --------- |
| GCC   | A   | +0.225             | 0.0571            | 0.871                |           |
| ACC   | T   | +0.311             | 0.1143            | 0.938                |           |
| CTC   | L   | +0.338             | 0.2000            | 0.938                |           |
| AAG   | K   | +0.136             | 0.3429            | 0.948                |           |
| GGA   | G   | +0.118             | 0.3429            | 0.948                |           |

```{=latex}
\needspace{14\baselineskip}
```

### E site — Depleted (day_10 < day_5)

| codon | aa  | effect (`log2_FC`) | raw p (`p_value`) | adjusted p (`p_adj`) | flag      |
| ----- | --- | ------------------ | ----------------- | -------------------- | --------- |
| ATT   | I   | -0.304             | 0.0286            | 0.871                | floor     |
| GTG   | V   | -0.480             | 0.0571            | 0.871                | low-count |
| ACG   | T   | -0.465             | 0.0571            | 0.871                | low-count |
| GCG   | A   | -0.593             | 0.1143            | 0.938                | low-count |
| GAT   | D   | -0.195             | 0.1143            | 0.938                |           |

### Flag glossary

- `floor` — the row sits at the test's discrete minimum possible raw p-value, given the sample size. For MW two-sided at n=4 vs n=4 this floor is exactly 0.02857. In a 61-codon per-site BH family, one floor hit BH-corrects to ~= 1.74 (cannot reach `p_adj` < 1 on its own); the A-site's observed `p_adj` = 0.436 arises only because 4 floor hits tie at the same site, giving 61 x 0.0286 / 4 = 0.436.
- `nominal-only` — raw p < 0.05 but `p_adj` >= 0.05. At n=4 vs n=4 the smallest attainable raw p is the floor itself (0.02857), so the only raw p < 0.05 rows are floor rows; they carry `floor`, not `nominal-only`. No row in this file qualifies for `nominal-only` (the next discrete raw p above the floor is 0.0571, which is already above 0.05).
- `low-count` — `min(median_day_10, median_day_5) < 0.005`; the `log2_FC` column for this row is dominated by single-rep fluctuations and should not be over-read (verifiable via `python scripts/dylan_table_checker.py <CSV> --family wilcoxon`). At codon level the rare-codon median frequencies in this file include CCT (~0.1%), GCG, TTT (~0.5%), CGG, and CTA all below ~0.5%.

## Numbers at a glance

- `n_tests`: 183
- `n_significant` (adjusted-p < 0.05): 0
- `n_significant` (adjusted-p < 0.10): 0
- `min adjusted-p`: 0.436 (site A, BH wall for 4 floor rows in 61-codon family: 61 x 0.0286 / 4)
- `min raw-p`: 0.02857 (= MW exact floor); 7 rows at the floor — A: CAA, GGA, TCT, TTT; E: ATT; P: CTT, GGA
- `p_floor`: 0.02857 (n=4 vs n=4 two-sided exact)
- Per-site BH families:
  - A site: 61 tests, 0 hits at p_adj<0.05, min p_adj = 0.436 (4 floor rows)
  - P site: 61 tests, 0 hits at p_adj<0.05, min p_adj = 0.581 (2 floor rows)
  - E site: 61 tests, 0 hits at p_adj<0.05, min p_adj = 0.871 (1 floor row)

## Biological interpretation

**The file shows no statistically significant differences at FDR<0.05 across all 183 codon-level day_10-vs-day_5 comparisons; the closest any site gets is min `p_adj` = 0.436 at the A-site (concrete).** Raw-p is clamped at 0.02857 (the n=4 vs n=4 MW exact floor) and the 61-codon per-site BH family puts the wall for a single floor hit at ~= 1.74 — i.e. no lone floor row can reach `p_adj` < 1 by itself. The A-site's observed min `p_adj` = 0.436 is exactly the BH wall for **4 floor rows tied at one site** (61 x 0.0286 / 4). As the `larger-bh-family` caveat notes, the aa-resolution d10-vs-d5 sister reaches `p_adj` = 0.381 with only 1 floor + 2 near-floor rows, so codon resolution here structurally lives further from FDR<0.05 than aa resolution — 0.436 is a structural ceiling, not biological FDR significance.

**A-site shows a coherent 4-floor-row block at d10-vs-d5: CAA / TCT / TTT depletion + GGA enrichment (exploratory).** All four sit at `p_adj` = 0.436 (the A-site BH wall). The signature reads as day_10 reps trending toward more A-site GGA (Gly, +0.317) and less A-site CAA (Gln, -0.681), TCT (Ser, -0.281), TTT (Phe, -0.465) than day_5 reps. Arg also leans day_10-enriched at A through two synonyms in the displayed table — CGT (+0.367, raw p 0.0571) and CGC (+0.250, raw p 0.1143) — though both sit above the floor. None survives FDR; all are exploratory and worth cross-checking against the aa-resolution sister `between_timepoint_wilcoxon_d10_vs_d5_aa.csv` (to see whether these per-codon shifts aggregate to an amino-acid-level signal) and against `timepoint_fisher_within_condition_d10_vs_d5_codon`, named in Methods as the test that isolates the within-condition day_5 to day_10 move.

**P-site echoes the A-site GGA enrichment with its own GGA floor row, in the same direction (exploratory).** P-GGA (+0.366, floor, `p_adj` = 0.581) and A-GGA (+0.317, floor, `p_adj` = 0.436) are both day_10-enriched, so within this report the Gly-up signal at the two sites concentrates on a single synonym (GGA) rather than spreading across Gly as a class — the other rows in the displayed A- and P-site tables are different codons. Whether this is genuine Gly-codon biology or two coincident floor rows cannot be settled here; the within-condition Fisher sister named in Methods (`timepoint_fisher_within_condition_d10_vs_d5_codon`) is the natural cross-check. The E-site contributes only one floor row (ATT -0.304, `p_adj` = 0.871) and is otherwise flat; with no second corroborating row in this report it is most naturally read as a discrete-floor coincidence.

**The most extreme |log2_FC| cells in every panel are `low-count` rare codons (exploratory).** The largest-magnitude bars at the tails of all three panels are rare codons. A-site CTA and E-site CGG have no Top-hits row at all — they are named among the sub-0.5% rare codons in the flag glossary and annotated `low-count` in the individual-plot captions at the foot of this report. The only one of these tail codons that carries the `low-count` flag in a displayed Top-hits table is the P-site TTT depletion (-0.659) in the P-site depleted block. Per the `low-count-rare-codon-instability` caveat, codons below the `min(median_day_10, median_day_5) < 0.005` threshold have |log2_FC| dominated by single-rep frequency moves — read these tail magnitudes as sampling noise, not mechanism. The A-site floor block is more stable by contrast: of the four A-site floor rows, only TTT carries the `low-count` flag in the displayed table; CAA, TCT and GGA do not, so the A-site signature is not purely a rare-codon artefact.

**What to carry forward.** Concrete claim to quote: this file shows no signal at FDR<0.05 for day_10-vs-day_5 at codon resolution with conditions pooled; the closest approach is min `p_adj` = 0.436 at site A, driven by 4 floor rows tied there. Exploratory patterns worth checking in sister tests: (1) does the coordinated A-site + P-site GGA enrichment reproduce in `timepoint_fisher_within_condition_d10_vs_d5_codon` within BWM or control individually (the within-condition Fisher named in Methods is the design that can separate conditions)? (2) does the largest-|effect| A-site floor row, CAA depletion (-0.681), reproduce in that same within-condition sister, or is it averaged out by the condition pooling flagged in the `condition-pooled-confound` caveat? (3) does the d5-vs-d0 codon contrast (`between_timepoint_wilcoxon_d5_vs_d0_codon.csv`) show opposite-sign movement on these same A-site cells, which would be consistent with a transient day_5 dip readable across the family? Noise to ignore: every `low-count` plot-tail cell (A-CTA, E-CGG, and the P-site TTT magnitude) — those |log2_FC| values are not numbers to quote.

## Methods

Mann-Whitney U / Wilcoxon rank-sum two-sided on per-replicate codon frequencies, n=4 day_10 (BWM_day10_rep2/3 + control_day10_rep2/3) vs n=4 day_5 (BWM_day5_rep2/3 + control_day5_rep2/3), BH-FDR per site (each A/P/E site = 61-codon family). Effect column is `log2_FC` of medians; test statistic is `U_stat`. The test answers "do day_10 and day_5 reps differ in per-rep codon frequency at this site?", with BWM and control reps pooled within each timepoint. Does *not* answer "BWM-vs-control at any single day" (that is `per_timepoint_fisher_codon`) and does *not* answer "BWM moves from day_5 to day_10 holding condition fixed" (that is `timepoint_fisher_within_condition_d10_vs_d5_codon`).

## What the caveats mean for reading this file

- **mw-floor-blocking.** Raw-p is clamped at 0.0286 (n=4 vs n=4 floor); for a 61-codon per-site BH family, a lone floor hit BH-corrects to ~= 1.74, and even 4 tied floor hits at one site only reach `p_adj` = 0.436. "0/183 at FDR<0.05" here is the design's structural ceiling, not biological evidence that day_10 ~= day_5.
- **condition-pooled-confound.** Each timepoint's n=4 is 2 BWM + 2 control reps; opposite-direction BWM and control time responses cancel inside this MW. Use `timepoint_fisher_within_condition_d10_vs_d5_codon` to disentangle (which is where the A/P-GGA signal is corroborated as shared across conditions).
- **n=4-low-power.** Low power; the no-signal result is weakly informative.
- **p-floor-aware-headline.** Headline above explicitly flags 0.0286 as the raw-p floor and the structural source of "no FDR hits".
- **weighted_log2_enrichment-absent.** `log2_FC` of medians only; rare codons (CCT ~0.1%, GCG, TTT, CGG, CTA ~0.5%) get the same column real estate as common codons (CAA, GGA, AAA) and dominate the |effect| tails.
- **larger-bh-family.** 61 codons per site -> BH wall ~3x more stringent than aa. The aa-level d10_vs_d5 sister reached p_adj = 0.381 with 1 floor + 2 near-floor rows; this file gets to p_adj = 0.436 only because 4 site-A floor rows tied — codon resolution structurally lives further from FDR<0.05 than aa resolution despite finer dynamic range.
- **low-count-rare-codon-instability.** Top |effect| at E (CCT +0.596 at median freq ~0.1%) and P (TTT -0.659 at median freq ~0.5%) sit in the regime where small absolute count moves dominate. Treat |log2_FC| > 0.5 on these as sampling noise unless they reproduce in a sister test.

### Considered and ruled out

- **asymptotic-with-ties.** Concern that scipy may default to the asymptotic Z approximation under tied zero-frequency reps for rare codons, which would shift p-values away from the exact-floor bound — a higher risk here than for the n=6 between-condition file because the smaller n=4-per-arm sample and codon-resolution rare counts compound. **Ruled out, empirically verified** (see audit in `scripts/_for_claude_mw_branch_audit.py`, invoked as `python scripts/_for_claude_mw_branch_audit.py --design between_timepoint --timepoints day10 day5 --level codon`): only 2 of 183 (site, codon) tests have any rank ties in the pooled 8-element sample — both rare codons (CGG@E, TTA@P), both far from raw p<0.05 (smallest tied-test p = 0.124 at TTA@P). For those 2, `scipy.stats.mannwhitneyu(method='auto')` chose the **asymptotic** branch; for the remaining 181 it chose **exact**. Recomputed p-values match the pipeline CSV to ~8e-17, so the pipeline's reported p-values are already the right scipy auto-branch choice (181 exact + 2 asymptotic). As a counterfactual, forcing `method='asymptotic'` for all 183 tests shifts raw p-values by at most 0.076 (median 0.006), flips **zero** raw-p<0.05 decisions, and leaves the per-site BH conclusion unchanged: 0 hits at FDR<0.05 either way, with the asymptotic counterfactual barely moving min p_adj (A/P/E ~= 0.46 / 0.62 / 0.91 vs the as-shipped 0.44 / 0.58 / 0.87). The branch choice does not affect any FDR-level conclusion in this file.

*(No further per-CSV caveats apply here.)*

---

## Plots — individual

![A-site day_10-vs-day_5 codon bar plot. X-axis is the 61 sense codons ordered by log2_FC high-to-low (left: CGT, GGA, CGC, GGG, ACG, GCC, ACC, TGG, TGC, TTA, TCG, TTC, TAC, TCC, ...; right: ... CTT, GCA, AAA, TAT, GCG, TTT, CGA, CAA, CTA). Y-axis is log2_FC ranging ~-1.05 to +0.37. Largest enrichments at CGT (~+0.37) and GGA (~+0.32); largest depletions at CTA (~-1.05, `low-count`), CAA (~-0.68, floor), CGA (~-0.58), TTT (~-0.47, floor). Floor rows visible mid-pack at GGA on the enriched side and at CAA / TCT / TTT on the depleted side.](../../plots/between_timepoint/d10_vs_d5/codon/individual/site_A_codon_Day10_vs_Day5_barplot.png)

![P-site day_10-vs-day_5 codon bar plot. X-axis is the 61 sense codons ordered by log2_FC high-to-low (left: CGA, AGG, GGA, CGG, ATA, ACC, AGT, CCC, CGC, GAG, GCG, CCT, AGC, TGC, CGT, GGG, ...; right: ... TGG, ACA, ATT, CAA, ACG, TTT). Y-axis is log2_FC ranging ~-0.66 to +0.61. Largest enrichment at CGA (~+0.61, not in top hits — `low-count` Arg codon territory); largest depletions at TTT (~-0.66, `low-count`), ACG (~-0.64), CAA (~-0.56). Two floor rows visible mid-pack: GGA (+0.366) on the enriched side and CTT (-0.189) on the depleted side — floor flag tracks raw-p, not effect size.](../../plots/between_timepoint/d10_vs_d5/codon/individual/site_P_codon_Day10_vs_Day5_barplot.png)

![E-site day_10-vs-day_5 codon bar plot. X-axis is the 61 sense codons ordered by log2_FC high-to-low (left: CCT, CTC, CCA, ACC, CGC, CCC, GCC, ACA, GGG, AGA, TCC, AAG, GGA, TGT, GTC, ...; right: ... AGG, AGC, TAT, TTT, ACG, GTG, CCG, TCG, GTA, GCG, CGG). Y-axis is log2_FC ranging ~-1.18 to +0.60. Largest enrichment at CCT (~+0.60, `low-count`); largest depletions at CGG (~-1.18, `low-count`), GCG (~-0.59, `low-count`), GTA, TCG, CCG (all `low-count` Pro/Thr/rare-codon territory). One floor row visible mid-pack at ATT on the depleted side. Site E carries the broadest dynamic range of the three panels here but most of it is rare-codon noise.](../../plots/between_timepoint/d10_vs_d5/codon/individual/site_E_codon_Day10_vs_Day5_barplot.png)
